bugs.error_message: Deploy preflight takes 5-6 min per invocation; PHP lint walks vendor/
bugs.root_cause: find at line 206 walked all .php under THEME_DIR with no prunes. 3,881 files total but only 124 are the theme's; the other 3,756 are Composer vendor deps. At 0.36s per php -l, vendor-lint alone cost 5-6 min per deploy. vendor/ was already gitignored, excluded from tarball, and excluded from phpcs — the lint walk was the last path that didn't get the memo.
bugs.fix: Added -prune for vendor, node_modules, tests, .git. Replaced hardcoded /opt/homebrew/bin/php with `command -v php` fallback. Added file count to success log. Scope: 124 (was 3,881). Expected preflight: ~45s (was ~5-6 min).

bugs.error_message: No concurrency guard; two deploys can race on the remote .old.$swap_id rename
bugs.root_cause: Hot-swap uses a timestamp+PID swap_id for rename-aside-and-rename-into-place. Two parallel deploys produce different swap_ids but race on the same live path. If deploy B's rename lands between A's rename-aside and rename-into-place, A loses its backup anchor and B's backup points at A's half-extracted tarball.
bugs.fix: Added acquire_deploy_lock() using /tmp/skyyrose-deploy.lock with PID stored inside. Stale lock (PID dead) reclaimed; live lock refuses second deploy with exit 2. release_deploy_lock fires in cleanup trap.

bugs.error_message: If verify_live() fails, site stays broken; no auto-rollback
bugs.root_cause: verify_live returned non-zero but main() only logged and exited 1 — the broken theme remained live. Old theme was already deleted by the immediate rm -rf .old.$swap_id at end of try_rsync, so no backup to restore.
bugs.fix: Added auto_rollback() that SSHs remote, reverses the swap (mv broken to .failed.$ts, restore .old.$ts into live path), and flushes caches. Changed try_rsync to NOT delete .old.$ts immediately. Cleanup trap inspects ROLLBACK_REQUESTED and calls auto_rollback on verify failure. Exit code 3 signals rollback occurred.

bugs.error_message: .old.$swap_id deleted immediately after swap; no rollback anchor
bugs.root_cause: Original compound command chained `mv new -> live && rm -rf .old.$swap_id`. Bounded disk but destroyed the only artifact auto-rollback could restore from.
bugs.fix: Removed the immediate rm. Added deterministic retention: after every swap, ls .old.* on remote and xargs rm -rf all but the 2 most recent. Bounded disk, rollback anchor preserved.

bugs.error_message: verify_live checked only homepage; CSS/JS breakage silent when WP-CDN cached HTML
bugs.root_cause: verify_live() only curled the homepage. If WP-CDN served cached HTML but new CSS/JS 404'd from origin, check passed while site was visibly broken. Also: no per-phase timing, no structured log.
bugs.fix: Extended verify_live() to curl style.css, immersive.css, immersive.js with cache-busting. Every asset URL must return 200. Added DEPLOY_CHECK_ASSETS env var for project-specific additions. Added phase_start/phase_end/phase_summary (bash 3.2 compatible scalars — no associative arrays). Tee'd stdout+stderr to /tmp/skyyrose-deploy-<ts>.log.

bugs.error_message: tar -czf wastes CPU compressing already-compressed media (WebP/GLB/JPG/PNG)
bugs.root_cause: Theme payload ~380 MB, of which ~316 MB is already-compressed binary media. gzip achieves ~0% reduction on those formats but costs single-threaded CPU per deploy.
bugs.fix: Runtime-detect tar --zstd and zstd binary. When available, use `tar --zstd -cf` (5x faster, similar ratio on text parts). Fallback to plain `-cf` (no compression) when zstd absent — still faster than gzip on pre-compressed media. Matching remote extract flag. Tarball extension adjusts.

bugs.error_message: SSHPASS exported for entire deploy duration, leaking credential to every child process
bugs.root_cause: build_ssh_cmd() unconditionally exported SSHPASS as soon as the script started; remained in env for preflight, tar, every subprocess until exit. On shared systems, `ps e <pid>` or /proc/<pid>/environ leak the password.
bugs.fix: Moved SSHPASS export from build_ssh_cmd() into try_rsync() just before scp. Added `unset SSHPASS` immediately after remote extract. Exposure window shrunk from entire deploy (~7 min) to sub-second. No functional change — sshpass -e reads from env at invocation.

bugs.error_message: 43.4 MB orphan GIF bloating every deploy payload for no reason
bugs.root_cause: GIF (1920x1080, 326 frames) superseded by rose-gold-spin.mp4 (3.86 MB) and rose-gold-spin.webm (2.34 MB) on 2026-04-14 but source GIF never removed. Zero references anywhere in theme PHP/CSS/JS/JSON. Every deploy paid the tar + upload cost for a file that would never be served.
bugs.fix: Pre-delete grep confirmed zero references. Deleted the .gif. MP4/WebM remain as modern animated-logo assets. Savings: 43.4 MB off every future tarball (11% payload reduction).

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: DEPLOY_LOCK_FD should be DEPLOY_LOCK_HELD

bugs.root_cause: value: 'lh-004', label: 'Love Hurts Varsity Jacket', collection: 'love-hurts' → 'lh-006', label: 'The Fannie', collection: 'love-hurts'
bugs.fix: Changed value: 'lh-004', label: 'Love Hurts Varsity Jacket', collection: 'love-hurts' → 'lh-006', label: 'The Fannie', collection: 'love-hurts'

bugs.error_message: front-page.php uses wc_get_products() to count products per collection — breaks when WooCommerce categories are not synced to CSV catalog
bugs.root_cause: wc_get_products() queries WC product categories, which are separate from the CSV catalog. Category sync is not guaranteed. Result: count is 0 when WC categories don't exist.
bugs.fix: Replaced wc_get_products() block with count(skyyrose_get_collection_products($col['slug'])) — reads directly from CSV catalog helper

bugs.error_message: template-preorder-gateway.php: (a) missing kids-capsule collection, (b) $fallback_products uses nonexistent SKUs (br-hoodie, lh-hoodie, sg-hoodie etc.), (c) wc_get_products() query with fallback to fake data, (d) card args missing image_url and correct permalink
bugs.root_cause: Template was written before CSV catalog helpers existed. Fallback products were placeholder data with invented SKUs never in the catalog. kids-capsule was simply omitted. WC_Product branch was dead code once WC categories weren't populated.
bugs.fix: Removed $fallback_products array and wc_get_products() block entirely. Added kids-capsule to $collections. Replaced product loading with skyyrose_get_collection_products() per slug. Fixed card args: image_url uses skyyrose_product_image_uri(front_model_image|image), permalink uses skyyrose_product_url($sku), price uses skyyrose_format_price($item). Removed WC_Product instanceof branch (catalog always returns arrays).

bugs.error_message: TypeError: e.target.closest is not a function — SVG elements and other non-Element nodes dispatched to document-level event listeners don't have .closest()
bugs.root_cause: SVG child elements (path, circle, use, etc.) are SVGElement not HTMLElement and may not implement .closest() in older browsers. document-level capture listeners receive events from any element on the page including SVG internals.
bugs.fix: Added typeof guard before every .closest() call: getCardData(), mouseenter listener, mouseleave listener, click listener — each now checks !el || typeof el.closest !== 'function' before calling.

bugs.error_message: 404.php references skyyrose-mascot-reference.png which does not exist in assets/images/mascot/ — only skyy-canonical.jpeg exists
bugs.root_cause: 404.php used an old filename (skyyrose-mascot-reference.png) that was never created. The canonical mascot file is skyy-canonical.jpeg.
bugs.fix: Replaced both references (path and URL) to skyyrose-mascot-reference.png with skyy-canonical.jpeg in the mascot fallback block of 404.php.

bugs.error_message: Tripo3D pipeline aborts: no key+region combo has credits
bugs.root_cause: Single TRIPO_API_KEY in env is bound to .ai region with $0.00 balance; .com region returns 401 for the same key (Tripo accounts are region-scoped, not key-scoped). Memory says user maintains multiple accounts but only one key is in env.
bugs.fix: Either top up the existing .ai account or set TRIPO_API_KEYS=key1,key2 (csv) so discover_credit_holding_account() can find a credit-holding combo across all (key x region) pairs. Script already supports csv via TRIPO_API_KEYS env.

bugs.error_message: Tripo riggability check returns riggable=False (no message) for skyy-canonical.jpeg-derived mesh
bugs.root_cause: Tripo BIPED auto-rigger needs visible anatomical landmarks (distinct elbow joints, separable arms, clear neck-shoulder transition). Skyy canonical reference is a Pixar chibi mascot: ~3-head proportions, right hand IN jacket pocket (arm merges with torso), curly hair shell occluding neck. BIPED detection fails silently with riggable=False, message="". Cost burned per failed attempt: ~310 credits (~$2.50) for the mesh generation step.
bugs.fix: PRIMARY: rig manually via Mixamo (free, mixamo.com) — Adobe rigger tolerates chibi proportions and produces Mixamo-named skeleton + idle/walk presets that skyy-3d.js already expects. Use existing mesh at generated_assets/3d/skyy-rig/mesh/567f76ce-3368-4aa9-81f3-b51e3b55b7f5_pbr.glb. ALT: regenerate reference image as a clean T-pose (arms fully out, no pocket-hand) before retrying Tripo.

bugs.error_message: chromadb.types.validate_where: 'Expected where to have exactly one operator, got {color: green, _namespace: A}'
bugs.root_cause: ChromaDB requires Where clauses to have exactly one top-level operator. Merging filter_metadata + namespace into a flat dict is rejected by Chroma when both are present.
bugs.fix: Wrap multi-key filter+namespace combos with $and: {"$and": [filter_metadata, {"_namespace": ns}]}. Single-key paths (only filter, only namespace, neither) stay flat.

bugs.error_message: voyageai.error.RateLimitError: 10K TPM / 3 RPM cap on free tier (no payment method)
bugs.root_cause: Voyage free tier (no payment method) is throttled to 10K tokens/min and 3 requests/min. The 33-SKU SkyyRose catalog totals ~32K-44K tokens, so a single embed_batch call exceeds the TPM ceiling.
bugs.fix: Two paths: (A) add payment method on https://dashboard.voyageai.com/ to unlock standard limits — free 200M tokens still apply for voyage-3 series; (B) throttle indexing to <10K tokens per minute via batched submission with time.sleep(60) between batches. The 200M free-token budget is generous; payment method is the simplest unblock.
